title: BEAM Therapeutics Expands Liver-Targeted Genetic Disease Program
text: Beam Therapeutics announced a new liver-targeted genetic disease program, BEAM-304, for the treatment of phenylketonuria (PKU), a disease affecting approximately 20,000 individuals in the U.S. with significant unmet need. BEAM-304 combines clinically validated base editing technology with internally discovered and optimized lipid nanoparticle delivery, with potential to bring forward one-time, durable treatments for majority of PKU patients. Beam advances BEAM-304 using innovative development approach in which multiple mutation-specific base editors are developed efficiently within single clinical program. Initial clinical development focuses on base editors addressing two most prevalent variants found in nearly half of U.S. PKU patients. BEAM-304 will be evaluated in Phase 1/2, open-label, dose exploration and dose expansion clinical trial investigating safety, tolerability, pharmacodynamics, pharmacokinetics and efficacy. Investigational New Drug filing anticipated in 2026.
